title: The Wednesday Waggle
text:
The Wednesday Waggle is an email newsleter published every Wedneseday during term time summarising opportunities in Computing and AI for:
.. , with occasional newsletters going out during non-teaching periods.
If there is a career related event or a job opportunity you’d like to tell other students about via the next Waggle, tell us about it and we’ll pass it on bit.ly/waggle-it.1

title: The Wednesday Waggle
section: Not studying CS? Add yourself to the mailing list
text:
Not studying CS? Add yourself to the mailing list
If you’re studying subjects other than Computer Science (CS) at the University of Manchesetr, but have an interest in opportunities in Computing and AI, you can add yourself to the Wednesday Waggle mailing list at bit.ly/waggle-me.2

title: The Wednesday Waggle
text:
Employers: Add your vacancies etc
If you’re an employer who’d like to include a vacancy, event or opportunity to the next newsletter, please fill in this form at forms.cloud.microsoft/e/1y4XTERggU
If you’re interested in reaching a wider audience beyond Computer Science, see employers.manchester.ac.uk

title: The Wednesday Waggle
text:
The Wednesday What?
The Wednesday What? The Wednesday Waggle. Bees have been a symbol of Manchester since the industrial revolution, symbolising community (the hive) and industry (work ethic). Just like the waggle dance of bees tells the hive mind where the nectar, pollen and other resources are located, this newsletter can help students like you find promising opportunities for investigation.
